# Phase 10.0.17 Existential Safeguards Plan

Status: Planning-only. This plan does not create executable workflows, runtime authority, provider authority, infrastructure authority, deployment authority, autonomous governance, APIs, background jobs, emergency-governance override systems, autonomous crisis-authority systems, or execution authority.

## Objective

Preserve constitutional continuity, emergency restraint, anti-panic governance, catastrophic escalation containment, and fail-safe civilization safeguards after wisdom and prudence doctrine.

## In-Scope Documents

- Constitutional existential-risk doctrine.
- Civilization catastrophic-failure containment philosophy.
- Institutional emergency-restraint framework.
- Constitutional safeguard survivability architecture.
- Anti-panic governance doctrine.
- Civilization fail-safe continuity standards.
- Catastrophic escalation containment framework.
- Governance emergency humility philosophy.
- Constitutional crisis-containment survivability doctrine.
- Institutional resilience under extreme pressure standards.
- Phase 10.0.17 existential safeguard summary.

## Validation Checklist

- No runtime activation exists.
- No execution authorization exists.
- No deployment authorization exists.
- No provider onboarding exists.
- No orchestration activation exists.
- No infrastructure activation exists.
- No existential-safeguard layer creates runtime authority.
- No crisis review bypasses constitutional supremacy.
- No emergency-restraint layer enables hidden execution escalation.
- No safeguard framework weakens auditability.

## Review Notes

- Existential safeguards remain human-reviewed, auditable, non-runtime, non-operational, anti-panic, and anti-override.
- Emergency restraint preserves constitutional boundaries during crisis.
- Safeguards cannot authorize their own override.
- Do not include: runtime enablement, providers, adapters, deployment paths, credentials, OAuth, APIs, fetch/axios, Prisma/Supabase changes, background jobs, autonomous governance, emergency-governance override systems, autonomous crisis-authority systems, or execution systems.
